Do Not Hire Robert Ponce!

I had an awful experience with Robert Ponce as my attorney. As a 24-year-old single mother needing legal help, I trusted him, only to be completely mislead and taken advantage of. From the start, he misled me, claiming he was “reducing” a felony charge to a misdemeanor. Later, I found out it had only ever been a misdemeanor—there was no felony to “reduce.”

Throughout the entire process, I never even got the chance to physically speak to Robert Ponce himself. Every conversation I had was with his front desk assistant. Looking back, that should’ve been a red flag, but I wanted to trust that he was handling my case properly and doing what was best for myself and my child. Instead, he dragged out my case over nearly a year, ultimately charging me over $3,500 for what I now realize was a complete lie.

When he asked if I wanted to keep him on for further representation, I declined due to his high fees and my lack of confidence in his help. I informed him that I was low on funds, hoping he might understand. But instead, he immediately handed my case over to a public defender without even notifying me of an upcoming court date. This lack of communication resulted in me missing the appearance and having a warrant issued for my arrest. When I finally got back on track with the public defender, they were shocked he hadn’t told me about the court date, putting me in an unnecessary and stressful situation.

Robert Ponce’s lack of honesty, communication, and compassion shows he’s only interested in adding another client to his roster to squeeze out a few thousand dollars. His business practices are extremely shady, and it’s a shame there are lawyers out there who operate like this. I would never recommend him to anyone, and I strongly advise anyone considering his services to look elsewhere. Don’t let him take advantage of you the way he did to me.

Avoid James Alderson at All Costs!

My experience with James Alderson as my attorney was an absolute nightmare. As a 24-year-old single mother needing legal help to navigate a custody situation, I trusted him, only to be completely taken advantage of. Not only did he lack any real communication, but he also belittled me and made incredibly hurtful and unprofessional remarks. He even went so far as to call me “ignorant” and tried to instill fear by saying I shouldn’t have custody of my daughter and that she should be taken away—all because I slapped my ex-husband in the face after he made horrible remarks about me and my daughter. The disrespect and lack of empathy were shocking.

I initially hired James to help clarify custody details with my ex-husband, specifically regarding dates, holidays, and weekly schedules. But from the start, he twisted my trust. When I later informed his office that my ex-husband had filed a restraining order against me, I wasn’t seeking help for this—I only wanted him to be aware since he was my attorney. Despite this, James later claimed he “helped” the restraining order get dismissed (even though it had no grounds and wasn’t my reason for hiring him). When I reminded him that I’d only hired him for custody matters, he became dismissive, calling me “ignorant” and telling me he couldn’t represent me because of a misdemeanor on my record. He even suggested that I didn’t deserve custody of my daughter—an unbelievably hurtful and baseless statement.

In short, James Alderson took advantage of my situation as a young mother, provided no real support, and was entirely unprofessional and judgmental. His lack of communication, combined with his condescending and manipulative tactics, made a difficult situation even harder. I would never recommend him to anyone. If you’re seeking a compassionate, competent attorney, steer clear of James Alderson. Don’t let him take advantage of you as he did to me.

I recently had an incredibly frustrating experience with a law firm, and I feel it is my duty to share this publicly. I am deeply dissatisfied with how I was treated by Mr. Vondra and his firm. After receiving a "We finished your case" letter yesterday, along with a request for a Google review, I felt compelled to express my concerns.

Throughout the process, Mr. Vondra failed to return most of my calls or emails, leaving me in the dark about the status of my case. Despite my repeated inquiries, he chose to wrap up my case WITHOUT my consent or input. To add insult to injury, the letter I received boasted about his pride in his work and his referral-based business model, which I found laughable given his lack of communication and professionalism.

I specifically asked Mr. Vondra to negotiate my medical bills, yet when I contacted the billing department, they informed me that he NEVER even attempted to do so. It became clear that his primary concern was securing his fee, with little regard for my financial outcome. His promises were empty, and he was nowhere to be found when it came time to deliver results.

I am genuinely baffled as to how Mr. Vondra obtained his license to practice law. I cannot, in good conscience, recommend his services to anyone. Beware of this law firm and consider seeking legal counsel elsewhere.

I hired Heather June of 2020 for a Child Custody/DVRO case. She was always very professional and conducted herself well in the courtroom. I was pleased with her representing me because I thought all lawyers were like her. However, I have since been able to work with another lawyer about my same case. I quickly realized that not all operated the way Heather does. I know the saying goes, "A busy lawyer, is a good lawyer." Well, there is a point when too busy is NOT GOOD. I didn't know this while working with Heather but I know now that not all lawyers don't have time to return your calls, not all lawyers show up to hearings 30 minutes late and have to take you outside to get an update on the case, after clearly not looking at the case file since the last hearing (this happened every hearing).  I also know that not all lawyers neglect explaining court orders to their client. I am now very disappointed with the service provided by Heather and her team. I hate writing this because they are all so nice and seem to be very knowledgeable of family law. I only write this for other single parents seeking a Lawyer that will pay attention to their case and make sure they fight 100% whole heartedly. I am thankful that I now have a different lawyer that has already made a huge difference in my case. Hire Heather because she is very professional and knowledgeable. But please be sure she provides the proper service your case requires. I didn't know while I had her, or I would have held her more accountable. Best of luck to my fellow single parents seeking to honestly fight for what's best for their children.

In court, Kerrie was in the courtroom for my case, and I asked if they needed me too since my ex was brought in.  She said "not yet".  She comes out about 15 min later with my order and said "go ahead and sign this in front of the judge".  Assuming she had done everything she said she would do.  After I get home, I realize that they didn't even count a bunch of my ex's income and gave her a hardship deduction, which she shouldn't have received (according to Kerrie).   About 2 weeks later, I got a letter from child support services saying I was $2800 behind in support, and charging me interest!  I called Kerrie's office, and it took nearly a week for them to return my call, and another 2 weeks for me to be able to talk to someone.  The only advice I got was "well they could've given you more back support.  Kerrie charges $300 an hour, so is it worth it to meet with Kerrie?  You should go talk to child support services."  TOTAL WASTE OF MONEY!!

This woman literally allowed my ex husband, who has a great deal more money than I do, to walk all over me. Friends and family were shocked at the outcome of my case, to say the least. reviews.text:
I had the weirdest and most frustrating interaction with this law firm. A couple months ago, I put out a Yelp Project explaining my situation and hoping any lawyers could assist me with my particular case. I received a number of responses. All of the responses I received read something like "This isn't my area of expertise, but I here's someone that can possibly help." I'm sure there were other lawyers that saw the request, but simply didn't respond to the open request because it's not in their wheelhouse. Fast forward about two months (yesterday), I received a text from this law firm that said, "If you still need assistance, please call." I did. Biggest mistake ever! Since my situation is currently in limbo, I was curious if they could assist. When I called, I was greeted by a woman who after a few moments of discussion, tried to tell me that I need another type of attorney. Since I've been dealing with this situation for the last few months, have spoken to countless attorneys, reached out to the state BAR, used a third-party lawyer referral service AND even reached out to a service run by a prominent lawyer that has his own podcast, I was confident in telling this woman that "No, that is not the attorney I need." And then I began to get annoyed. I put the details of my case in my Yelp Project. Every attorney that reached out to me through it read the project information and responded by letting me know that they couldn't help me, but they offered me a lead that possibly could. The whole point of the Yelp Project is for people to reach out if they CAN help. Not if they can't. So why did I receive this text TWO MONTHS later asking me if I still needed help if they couldn't help me? I assumed this law firm had read the project!

The woman then kindly asked me if I wanted to talk to her supervisor. I agreed. The supervisor's name was Tina. Tina, almost immediately, tried to turn my inquiry into a battle of epic proportions. After explaining that I was confused about receiving a message on Yelp to call, she screeched into my ear "Well, we didn't say we could take your case!." "But WHY would you read the Yelp Project and message me if you COULDN'T take the case?!" I hollered back. Tina insisted on this line of reasoning involving them not saying they could take my case. It was reductive and fruitless. And I realized that this woman was absolutely asinine and wasting my time. So, I hung up and decided it's much better to warn others to not waste their time with this firm. If Tina is any representation of the lawyers that are part of this firm, you may want to reconsider unless you don't mind losing your case.

Not knowing anything about having to get a lawyer or how to go about the legal situation I was dealing with, made me extremely nervous and skeptical. But everyone that I was in communication with throughout my entire process was AMAZING. Specifically, Ashton and Tina. They were so informative, patient and most importantly reassuring. Having to get a lawyer is not cheap by any means…but this team worked with me and stayed in communication the whole time. At the end of my process, I felt so relieved and GRATEFUL I picked Greiner Law. 10/10 recommend. Thank you Greiner Law Corp team!!!!
